qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated orphan attachment uploads enable persistent storage exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about orphan attachment creation through POST /issues/attachments and missing staged-upload ownership or cleanup, not release deletion calling git tag -d on rel.TagName in internal/database/release.go.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No finalized qualifying finding identifies the advisory's release-deletion root cause. The only qualifying finding concerns authenticated orphan attachment staging and storage growth, whereas the advisory is a Git CLI option-injection flaw in release tag deletion.
evidence: The advisory names internal/database/release.go and the sink process.ExecDir(..., "git", "tag", "-d", rel.TagName) without --, with exploitation depending on a dash-prefixed tag name during release deletion.
The only finalized qualifying finding targets POST /issues/attachments plus attachment persistence and cleanup logic in repo/internal/route/repo/issue.go and repo/internal/database/attachment.go.
The finding does not describe release deletion, tag-name parsing, Git option injection, or any equivalent source-to-sink path involving rel.TagName.
